mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates increase the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, directly compressing equity valuations and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show positive mean returns when FEDFUNDS increases, or Rank IC remains below 0.02 over rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.2 percentage points signals deteriorating labor market conditions and reduced consumer spending capacity, driving negative equity returns over the subsequent 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns are positive in > 55% of triggered instances, or the signal loses statistical significance (p > 0.10) in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts indicates robust aggregate demand and construction sector activity, which boosts corporate revenue expectations and yields positive S&P 500 returns over a quarter.
failure_condition: The joint signal produces negative forward returns in > 60% of cases, or the interaction term fails to improve predictive accuracy over individual series in regression tests.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production reflects strengthening manufacturing output and supply-side capacity utilization, which triggers short-term momentum buying and positive S&P 500 returns within one week.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are negative during recovery phases when INDPRO accelerates, or the signal exhibits zero predictive power (Rank IC = 0) in recovery regimes.

mechanism_narrative: Elevated inflation combined with restrictive monetary policy forces central banks to maintain higher discount rates, eroding real corporate profits and generating negative equity returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive when both inflation and rates are elevated, or the interaction effect is statistically indistinguishable from zero in multivariate models.

mechanism_narrative: Consistent growth in total nonfarm payrolls expands the labor force and supports wage-driven consumption, which sustains corporate earnings growth and produces positive S&P 500 returns over a three-month horizon.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns are negative in > 50% of periods with payroll growth, or the series shows no monotonic relationship with equity returns across historical data.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly GDP growth exceeding 3.0% annualized confirms strong economic expansion, prompting immediate portfolio rebalancing into equities and generating positive S&P 500 returns within five trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are negative following strong GDP prints, or the threshold signal fails to separate positive from negative return periods with statistical significance.

mechanism_narrative: Declining industrial output alongside rising unemployment indicates a supply-demand mismatch and economic contraction, which triggers risk-off positioning and negative S&P 500 returns over the next month.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO declines and UNRATE rises simultaneously
failure_condition: The combined signal yields positive forward returns in > 65% of triggered instances, or the mechanism fails to predict return direction during documented recessionary periods.
